title: Semantic Reinforcement Memory runtime: migrate vector store from OpenSearch to S3 Vectors
description: Replace OpenSearch client path in VectorTopicMemory/Semantic Reinforcement Memory with S3 Vectors client operations for index, query, and metadata updates. Remove OpenSearch runtime dependency for this feature path and update config/env mappings.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Starting Task 2. Implemented new S3 Vectors client and rewired VectorTopicMemory config/runtime path to use s3_vectors.* keys and S3 vector store operations.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Validation: pytest -q plexus/reports/blocks/vector_topic_memory_test.py plexus/analysis/s3vectors_client_test.py -> 8 passed. Runtime path and new S3 vectors client tests are green.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Updated report templates and config loader to s3_vectors keys and removed OpenSearch package dependencies from pyproject for this feature path.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Updated report templates and config loader to s3_vectors keys and removed OpenSearch package dependencies from pyproject for this feature path.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Found and fixed runtime bug during live run: S3 Vectors GetIndex rejected requests when indexArn and bucket/index were both sent. Updated _vector_ref to send either indexArn OR bucket+index. Retested: 8 pytest cases pass.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Completed: runtime switched to S3 vectors client, config/env mappings updated to s3_vectors keys, templates updated, and targeted pytest suite green.
